skill.name: shadcn
skill.description: Manages shadcn components and projects — adding, searching, fixing, debugging, styling, and composing UI. Provides project context, component docs, and usage examples. Applies when working with shadcn/ui, component registries, presets, --preset codes, or any project with a components.json file. Also triggers for "shadcn init", "create an app with --preset", or "switch to --preset".
skill.summary: Manages shadcn components and projects — adding, searching, fixing, debugging, styling, and composin...
skill.author: shadcn

security_audit.summary: Audit complete. Static findings are false positives: backticks are markdown code formatting, URLs are documentation links, and cryptographic warnings are triggered by file paths. This is a legitimate UI component library skill with no actual security risks.

content.user_title: Build React UIs with shadcn components
content.value_statement: Developers struggle to maintain consistent, accessible UI components across React projects. This skill provides CLI commands, component composition patterns, and styling rules for building professional UIs with the shadcn/ui component library.
actual_capabilities: Add, search, and manage shadcn/ui components via CLI
Apply component composition patterns (Card, Dialog, Tabs, Forms)
Debug and fix common shadcn/ui implementation issues
Style components using semantic Tailwind tokens
Initialize new projects with presets and templates
Access component documentation and usage examples
limitations: Requires existing shadcn/ui project or init step first
Does not create new component designs from scratch
No runtime component library (source code only)
Limited to CLI-based workflows described in documentation

use_cases.title: Add components to React project
use_cases.description: Use CLI to add button, form, dialog, and other UI components to a Next.js or Vite project
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers building new React applications

use_cases.title: Debug styling and composition issues
use_cases.description: Fix common issues with z-index, variants, semantic colors, and component nesting

use_cases.title: Apply best-practice component patterns
use_cases.description: Follow official composition rules for forms, dialogs, tabs, and other complex components
use_cases.target_user: Teams establishing UI standards and patterns

prompt_templates.title: Add a new component
prompt_templates.prompt: Add the button and card components to my project using shadcn CLI. Show me the correct composition pattern for a settings card with a title, description, and action button.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to add new UI components

prompt_templates.title: Debug a component issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Debug and fix a dialog that is not appearing above other elements. The z-index seems wrong on mobile.
prompt_templates.description: Debug and fix a dialog that is not appearing above other elements. The z-index seems wrong on mobile.

prompt_templates.title: Search for a component
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the shadcn registry for a table component and add it with the correct installation command.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to find available components

prompt_templates.title: Style with semantic tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this button from hardcoded bg-blue-500 to use semantic tokens from the shadcn theme. Use the correct variant pattern.

output_examples.input: Add a dialog component with a form inside it
output: Composition pattern for accessible dialog with form

output_examples.input: Fix my form layout - fields are not spacing correctly
output: Replace div wrappers with FieldGroup + Field pattern
Apply correct variant for form validation states

best_practices: Always use project package runner (npx, pnpm dlx, bunx) based on project packageManager
Prefer component composition over custom markup (use Alert, Empty, Badge instead of styled divs)
Use semantic color tokens (bg-primary, text-muted-foreground) instead of raw Tailwind colors
anti_patterns: Do not override component colors with raw Tailwind classes - use variant prop instead
Do not use space-x-* or space-y-* - use flex with gap-* for layout
Do not invent CLI flags not documented in the official reference

faq.question: How do I initialize shadcn in a new project?
faq.answer: Run npx shadcn@latest init with your preferred template (next, vite, start).

faq.question: What package manager should I use?
faq.answer: Use the package manager detected in your project packageManager field - npx, pnpm, or bun.

faq.question: How do I add components?
faq.answer: Use npx shadcn@latest add <component-name> to install components from the registry.

faq.question: How do I customize component styles?
faq.answer: Use variant props and semantic tokens. Avoid raw Tailwind classes on components.

faq.question: What is the difference between radix and base components?
faq.answer: Radix components use asChild pattern, base components use render prop. Check npx shadcn@latest info.

faq.question: Can I use shadcn with any React framework?
faq.answer: Yes, supports Next.js, Vite, Create React App, React Router, and Astro templates.
